title: Song Of The Queer Green Frog
content:
"No, no, come and fly
        Through the sunny sky,
        Or honey sip
        From the rose's lip,
        Or dance in the air,
        Like spirits fair.
        Come away, come away;
        'Tis our holiday."
meaning:
Louisa May Alcott's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Song Of The Queer Green Frog"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
